title: Chapter 10 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Generics and Traits!

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Generics (`<T>`) in Rust?
options: To write reusable functions and structs that handle multiple data types without duplicating code
questions.explanation: Generics eliminate the need to write identical code for different types.

questions.question: Due to Monomorphization, using generic types in Rust slows down your program at runtime because the compiler has to figure out the type.
questions.explanation: Monomorphization generates exact, strongly-typed copies of your functions at compile time, guaranteeing zero runtime performance cost!

questions.question: If you are familiar with object-oriented languages like Java or C#, what Rust feature is most similar to an Interface?
questions.explanation: Traits strictly define required behavior (methods) that types must implement.

questions.question: To forcefully ensure that a generic type `<T>` uniquely implements specific required behavior before compiling securely perfectly legitimately explicitly cleanly correctly precisely appropriately cleverly smoothly expertly intelligently optimally exactly cleanly successfully intelligently fully effectively efficiently, you use a Trait ________.
